title: Portuguese Corn Flatbread
description: A traditional Portuguese dish featuring corn arepa filled with mozzarella cheese, served with a side of fry grind rice khao khuas, this recipe is kosher friendly and perfect for a cozy night in.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the cornmeal, salt, and sugar.
Add the beaten eggs to the bowl and stir until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place on a baking sheet.
Spread a quarter cup of mozzarella cheese on each disk, leaving a half inch border around the edges.
Top the cheese with a spoonful of fry grind rice khao khuas.
Fold the edges of the dough up over the filling to form a crust.
Brush the tops of the flatbreads with vegetable oil and bake for 20 minutes.
Remove the flatbreads from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before serving.

axes.primaryIngredient: corn arepa filled with mozarella cheese
